info.description:
Staging URL:     https://developer.api.stg.walmart.com/api-proxy/service/WPA/Api/v1/ 
Production URL : https://developer.api.walmart.com/api-proxy/service/WPA/Api/v1/ 

The Walmart Sponsored Products API enables programmatic access to campaign management and reporting, allowing you to automate, scale, and optimize your advertising. Our flexible solution will allow you to integrate with Sponsored Products in a way that meets your goals and objectives. The API functionalities include campaign and ad group creation, bid and budget adjustments, campaign scheduling, and performance reporting. 

Note: POST request to create new entities. PUT request should only include the attribute which needs to be updated and Id for which PUT request is fired for.
info.title: Walmart Sponsored Products API

get.description: This API allows you to retrieve all media by providing the required details in the request. Upon successful execution, the system returns a complete list of media resources for identification and management purposes.

parameters.description: The ID of the advertiser.
parameters.name: advertiserId

parameters.description: The unique ID of the media file generated from the notify media upload complete call. When an advertiser ID and multiple media IDs are provided, the service shall return the media details qualifying for the first media ID.
parameters.name: mediaId

parameters.description:
The value represents the status of the media file. When an advertiser ID and multiple status are provided, the service shall return the media details qualifying for the first status.

***Note:*** 
*- `PENDING` and `PROCESSING` status signify that the video is being processed at the backend for technical specification.*
*- `LIVED` status means that the video was associated with a campaign which went live and cannot be deleted.*
*- `AVAILABLE` signifies that the video is now available to be added to a campaign.*
*- `FAILED` signifies that the background process for video validation failed.*
parameters.name: status

The asOf field represents the date and time when the returned statistics (including ad spend, impressions, clicks, and daily remaining budget) were generated.

***Note:***

  *- Please review the `asOf` timestamp to confirm when the data was generated and determine if there’s any delay.*

dailyOutOfBudgetDatetime.description:
An indicator of whether a campaign has yet run out of its assigned daily budget or not.

It returns following values:
- Actual Date and Time: It returns date and timestamp of when the campaign exhausted its daily budget and stopped serving ads.
- Empty: It returns a blank if the daily budget is not yet exhausted

***Note:*** 
  *- Since it measures spend of daily budget, it only provides date and time stamp for the current day. Also, since it refreshes every day, it will return a blank the subsequent day if the daily budget is not exhausted.*
  *- The time zone is in PST and time stamp format is 24-hour format.*

dailyRemainingBudget.description: The amount of daily budget left after today's ad spend is consumed.
